title: orobanche minor subsp. crithmi
intro: Orobanche minor subsp. crithmi, also known as the crithm orobanche, is a rare and unique parasitic plant that belongs to the broomrape family. It is a type of hemiparasite that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of its host plant, typically Crepis crithmifolia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The crithm orobanche has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements to promote overall health and well-being.
